news.title: 2026 World Cup: Spain 1-0 Argentina (AET): Ferran Torres' extra-time winner secures Spain's second title
news.lede: Spain won their second World Cup title after Ferran Torres' 106th-minute smashed finish earned a 1-0 win against defending champions Argentina in the 2026 final. Spain were deserved winners. Torres scored with Spain's 20th shot while the game ended without Argentina registering a single shot on target. Lionel Messi touched the ball just 54 times in a match that took three hours to complete and featured a 27-minute half-time break.

news.title: 2026 World Cup: All the European sides' results
news.lede: Look back at all the European sides' results at the FIFA World Cup in Canada, Mexico and the United States. The 2026 FIFA World Cup in Canada, Mexico and the United States kicked off on 11 June and concluded with Spain's extra-time final victory against holders Argentina in East Rutherford on 19 July. Check out all the details of the European teams' matches. Wednesday 17 June: W 3-1 vs Jordan (San Francisco Bay Area) Monday 22 June: L 0-2 vs Argentina (Dallas) Sunday 28 June: D 3-3 vs Algeria (Kansas City) Thursday 2 July (R32): L 3-0 vs Spain (Los Angeles).

news.title: What’s left after this World Cup? A kind of messy hope | Jonathan Liew
news.lede: Kazakhs cheering Uzbeks, Bengalis wild for Argentina … This tournament was once a playground for nationalism. Now it shows how flimsy that allegiance is. It’s the World Cup final, so you pick a side. And don’t worry if you happen to be part of the 98.9% of the human race that is neither Spanish nor Argentinian – just make something up.

news.title: World Cup Golden Boot: Kylian Mbappé wins after Lionel Messi is held scoreless in final
news.lede: The stars were out throughout the 2026 World Cup, and they shined bright through the final days of the tournament. France's Kylian Mbappé was the last player to move out in front of the Golden Boot race, striking twice in Les Bleus' third-place match against England after they had stumbled to a 4-0 deficit in the first half Saturday. That put him two goals ahead of Lionel Messi before Argentina took the pitch in Sunday's final against Spain. Mbappé held on to win the Golden Boot, the award bestowed upon the World Cup's top goal scorer, after Messi was held scoreless in Argentina's 1-0 extra-time loss to Spain.

news.title: Gangs of New Jersey: how the World Cup final descended into ugly scenes between Argentina and Spain
news.lede: A blow-by-blow account of how players from both sides fought in the aftermath of the Sundau showpiece. The World Cup final took an ugly turn at the end of extra time as Spain were crowned champions. Here’s what happened, frame by frame. As the referee, Slavko Vincic, blows the full-time whistle, Spain’s substitutes run on to the pitch to celebrate World Cup glory with their teammates.

news.title: Spain’s metronome: the statistics behind Rodri’s superb World Cup campaign
news.lede: Midfielder’s supreme passing was too much for Argentina to handle in the final but his work without the ball proved just as crucial. Rodri is back to his best after multiple injury problems. The former Ballon d’Or winner has started only 22 games since rupturing his anterior cruciate ligament in September 2024 but you would have no idea from his performances at the World Cup that he had suffered so badly in the previous two years. The 30-year-old was already leading several statistical categories coming into the final on Sunday.

news.title: FIFA will reportedly investigate Argentina for brawl after World Cup final loss to Spain
news.lede: FIFA will reportedly be investigating Argentina for what happened after the whistle of the national team's 2026 World Cup final loss to Spain, per Sky Sports. After the final whistle of the match, which resulted in Spain winning 1-0, a brawl broke out between players. Tensions were running high throughout the match, and it came to a head after the game ended. Argentina's Leandro Paredes appeared to grab Spain's Eric García by the throat, and then other players were pushing, shoving and tripping.

news.title: The 2026 World Cup stars whose stock has risen this summer
news.lede: Few things in football can alter a player's reputation more than the World Cup. The biggest spotlight in football has again raised the stock of participating players. Here are six stars whose reputations have risen at the 2026World Cup. Just over two months ago, a petition calling forReal Madrid to sell Kylian Mbappe attracted more than 30million signatures.
